page: Amazon Web Services m6in.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage, but features a high baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m6in.12xlarge demonstrates solid mid-range performance across compute and memory tasks. It achieves a Geekbench multi-core score of 15823.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 48435.76. Notably, it shows strong performance in cryptographic and image processing workloads, scoring 43448.8 in the Passmark CPU encryption test and 15040.0 in Geekbench image processing, both placing in the top 25% tier. Memory bandwidth is measured at 158.1 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 69.22 GB/sec for large block write operations.

From a resource density and cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a balanced hardware profile for enterprise workloads requiring high network throughput without the overhead of local NVMe drives. The high baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps makes it highly efficient for distributed systems. It is well-suited for workloads such as database operations, static web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and moderate language model inference, where its 48 vCPUs and 192 GB of RAM can be fully utilized.
description: Amazon Web Services m6in.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 8375C processor. It features 48 vCPUs (24 physical cores) running at 3.5 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Lacking local storage, the instance relies on network-attached storage but compensates with a high baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate balanced performance, highlighted by strong results in cryptographic operations and image processing. With a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is qualitatively cost-effective for network-heavy enterprise applications, database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong cryptographic and image processing performance.

tagline: A network-optimized general purpose instance featuring forty-eight vCPUs, high-speed DDR4 memory, and seventy-five gigabits per second baseline bandwidth.

192.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS
Strong performance in cryptographic and image processing benchmarks
No local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage
Suited for database operations, web serving, and multi-threaded workloads
